title: Oral Glucose Gel
content_markdown:
##### Type

Oral glucose gel [no schedule]

##### Presentations

37.5g tube containing 15g glucose

##### Actions

- Glucose is the main energy source for the body’s cells, especially the brain 

- Glucose preparation is rapidly absorbed from oral / buccal mucosa 

- Causes an increase in blood glucose concentration

Onset: approximately 10 minutes.

#### Uses

Treatment of hypoglycaemia (BGL <4mmol/L) when patient is conscious and able to swallow

#### Contraindications

Impaired or absent swallow / gag reflex

#### Pregnancy and Breastfeeding

##### Pregnancy

- Safe to use

##### Breastfeeding

- Safe to use

#### Doses

##### Adult and Paediatric

- 1 x tube of 15g glucose orally 
- May be repeated once after 15 minutes if BGL still < 4 mmol/L, and level of consciousness still altered
- Total maximum dose 30g glucose
- Not to be given IV or IM.

#### Special Notes

- Patients should swallow the entire contents of the tube (where possible) to maximise the rise in BGL.
- Patients who respond to oral glucose gel should eat to prevent later development of further hypoglycaemia.
- Re-check BGL following treatment.
- Gel liquefies when over 30\xb0C (it is still useable). Re-cool if possible.
